briefing: Clues point to failed inter-subnet routing between office and archive LANs.
prompt: Inspect clues and install the device that restores traffic between 192.168.1.0/24 and 10.0.0.0/24.

pois.meaning_short: Missing L3 forwarding path between subnets.
Local L2 is healthy; gateway function is missing.
Host is reachable only if routing exists.

explain_short: Router is required for inter-subnet forwarding.
explain_full: Switch and bridge operate inside one L2 domain. This case requires L3 routing between two different /24 networks.

briefing: Collision and broadcast spikes indicate one oversized collision domain.
prompt: Pick the device that segments the LAN and reduces collision storms.

pois.meaning_short: Shared medium saturation points to L1/L2 bottleneck.
Flooded frames need MAC-based forwarding.

pois.log_line: Packet loss appears during office peak
pois.meaning_short: Segmentation with switching reduces contention.

explain_short: Switch isolates collisions and forwards by MAC table.
explain_full: Hub repeats frames to everyone. Switch learns MAC addresses and forwards traffic only to target ports.

briefing: Unknown unicast flooding appears around printer traffic bursts.
prompt: Install the device that stops frame flooding while keeping the same subnet.

pois.meaning_short: Frames are broadcast due to missing MAC learning.

pois.log_line: Queue spikes on shared segment during print jobs
pois.meaning_short: Traffic contention grows in non-switched segments.

pois.log_line: Hosts receive frames not addressed to them
pois.meaning_short: Selective forwarding is required.

explain_short: Switch learns MAC addresses and limits flooding.
explain_full: The issue is not routing. It is uncontrolled frame replication in one broadcast domain.

briefing: CRC errors and link flaps rise under load on a long cable run.
prompt: Choose the device that restores physical signal integrity on this segment.

pois.log_line: CRC errors rising on uplink cable
pois.meaning_short: Signal corruption occurs before higher-layer logic.
Physical regeneration is needed.
Problem is below L3 configuration.

explain_short: Repeater regenerates weakened electrical signal at Layer 1.
explain_full: L2/L3 devices do not fix damaged signal quality. This case is a physical link degradation.

briefing: Local pings work, but external addresses stay unreachable.
prompt: Select the device that provides default gateway routing to outside network.

pois.meaning_short: No path from LAN to external networks.
Internal switching works; external route is absent.
Need gateway routing beyond local subnet.

explain_short: Router provides default gateway and route selection.
explain_full: Without a router, hosts can only communicate inside the local subnet.

briefing: Two VLANs must exchange traffic; each VLAN is healthy alone.
prompt: Install the correct device for inter-VLAN communication.

pois.meaning_short: Intra-VLAN L2 works.
Issue is only across VLAN boundaries.

pois.log_line: Inter-VLAN ping blocked by policy path
pois.meaning_short: Needs Layer 3 gateway between VLANs.

explain_full: Switching alone keeps VLANs isolated. Router (or L3 switching) is required for inter-VLAN traffic.

briefing: Annex office link is too weak at distance and drops under burst load.
prompt: Pick the device that restores the long physical segment.

pois.meaning_short: Distance causes L1 attenuation.

pois.log_line: Frequent frame retries from annex side
pois.meaning_short: Physical errors trigger retransmissions.
Configuration is not the root cause.

explain_short: Repeater restores signal over long cable paths.
explain_full: The evidence indicates physical attenuation, not logical forwarding mistakes.

briefing: A temporary floor segment suffers heavy contention during meetings.
prompt: Choose the device that isolates endpoints and lowers collision pressure.

pois.meaning_short: Flooding must be constrained with MAC forwarding.
Shared-medium behavior indicates non-switched segment.

pois.log_line: Latency spikes only inside floor segment
pois.meaning_short: Access-layer segmentation is required.

explain_short: Switch provides dedicated collision domains per port.
explain_full: This is a classic access-layer contention case, solved by switching not by routing.

briefing: Archive subnet cannot reach update mirror in another subnet.
prompt: Install the correct forwarding device to cross subnet boundary.

pois.meaning_short: Local L2 domain works on source side.
Destination subnet is alive, but isolated.
A routing decision point is missing.

explain_short: Router is needed to forward between different /24 ranges.
explain_full: Bridge and switch stay in Layer 2 and cannot route across IP subnet boundaries.

briefing: Legacy lab segment reports frame flooding and duplicated traffic.
prompt: Pick the device that stabilizes this lab segment without changing subnet plan.

pois.log_line: Duplicate frames observed at multiple hosts
pois.meaning_short: Frames are not being selectively forwarded.

pois.log_line: Broadcast counters surge under file copy
pois.meaning_short: Broadcast containment is required at access edge.

pois.log_line: Routing table is unchanged and valid
pois.meaning_short: Problem is not route policy.

explain_short: Switching addresses frame flooding inside one subnet.
explain_full: Evidence indicates an L2 forwarding issue. Installing a switch resolves per-port forwarding behavior.
